Services

PowerShell Automation & Scripting

Custom PowerShell modules and scripts that add real value across your business — identity, services, data, reporting, workflows, and deployments.

PowerShell is the automation language of the Microsoft ecosystem. It reaches everything: Active Directory, Entra ID, Exchange, SharePoint, Azure, Intune, Windows Server, SQL Server, and any other system that exposes a COM interface, REST API, or WMI namespace. In the right hands, it is one of the most productive tools for eliminating operational toil in a Windows and Azure environment.

In the wrong hands, it is a collection of scripts that work in development, fail silently in production, can't be tested, and are abandoned the moment the person who wrote them leaves. RePass Cloud writes PowerShell automation that is production-grade: logged, tested, documented, and maintainable by a team rather than a single person.

What we build

PowerShell use cases

Identity & access management

Custom modules for Active Directory and Entra ID: bulk user provisioning and deprovisioning, group membership sync, password policy enforcement, stale account detection, access reviews, and delegation of administrative tasks to non-admin staff via constrained runspaces.

Windows Services management

Automated management of services.msc across fleets of servers: start/stop/restart orchestration, health monitoring, dependency-aware sequencing for multi-service application stacks, and alerting when services enter unexpected states.

Data management & ETL

Extract, transform, and load pipelines written in PowerShell for environments where lightweight, maintainable scripts outperform heavyweight ETL platforms. CSV, XML, JSON, SQL, and API-based data movement with error handling, logging, and retry logic.

Reporting & dashboards

Scheduled reports delivered via email, written to SharePoint, or exported to Excel and HTML. Licence utilisation, inactive account summaries, group membership audits, disk space trends, and any other operational metric that currently lives inside someone's head or a shared spreadsheet.

Workflow automation

Multi-step business processes encoded as PowerShell workflows: approval gates that write to ITSM tickets, conditional logic that routes work based on attributes, and scheduled jobs that execute against live systems with proper logging and rollback capability.

Deployments & release automation

Application and configuration deployments driven by PowerShell: packaging, pre-flight validation, environment-specific configuration injection, deployment sequencing, and post-deploy smoke tests — integrated with CI/CD pipelines or run as standalone scheduled tasks.

Infrastructure & endpoint management

OS configuration drift detection and remediation, Intune and GPO compliance reporting, software inventory, patch status, and device health monitoring — surfaced as actionable reports rather than raw event log noise.

Enterprise module development

Reusable, tested PowerShell modules built to enterprise standards: manifest versioning, Pester unit tests, comment-based help, pipeline support, and -WhatIf / -Confirm support. Modules your team can maintain and extend, not black-box scripts that only the original author understands.

Engineering standards

How we write PowerShell

Scripts that don't meet these standards aren't production automation — they're technical debt. Everything we deliver is written to the following baseline:

Idempotent — safe to run multiple times
Verbose logging to file and event log
Structured error handling and alerting
-WhatIf support for dry-run validation
Pester tests for critical logic
Secrets managed via Key Vault, not hardcoded
Cross-platform where required (PS 7+)
Comment-based help and inline documentation
Modern workplace

Automation across the Microsoft stack

The modern workplace is built on Microsoft technologies — and PowerShell is the connective tissue that holds the automation layer together. Where new products like Azure Logic Apps, Power Automate, or Azure Functions are the right fit, we use them. But many enterprise automation requirements need the flexibility, testability, and control surface that only direct PowerShell scripting provides.

We use PowerShell to bridge the gaps between systems that were never designed to talk to each other: the HR platform that exports a flat file at midnight, the Active Directory schema that hasn't been updated in ten years, the custom business application that only exposes a SQL view, and the modern Entra ID tenant that needs to reflect all of the above in near-real time.

The automation we deliver doesn't replace your team — it frees them from the repetitive, error-prone work so they can focus on the things that actually require human judgement.

Case Study · Enterprise / Retail

Identity Automation · 2,100 Employees

Rule-driven identity lifecycle across multiple AU/NZ brands. Zero daily manual IT intervention. Driven entirely by PowerShell automation against Active Directory and Microsoft 365.

PowerShell Active Directory M365
Case Study · Multi-brand Retail

Finance Workflow Automation

End-to-end petty cash processing automation via PowerShell, integrated with Active Directory, Entra ID, and Oracle HCM. Eliminated 4 FTE manual workload.

PowerShell Oracle HCM Entra ID
